methods.description: This simulation tested the assumption that the parameterisation of the glacier-climate model did not contribute to the discrepancy between glacial-geological and HadCM3 LGM climate anomalies. The process involved iteratively adjusting key model parameters (degree day factors for melt and glacier hypsometric profiles) while keeping boundary conditions fixed (INQUA LGM glacier database and HadCM3 LGM climate anomalies) until the model simulated equilibrium surface mass balance conditions. This method aimed to find optimized parameter values that reconcile the model output with the assumed correct boundary conditions.

steps.description: Assume the INQUA LGM glacier database (Ehlers and Gibbard, 2004a) and HadCM3 LGM climate anomalies were correct, using these as fixed boundary conditions for the simulation.
steps.searchable_summary: Load fixed boundary conditions for glacier model simulation from specified databases.
inputs: INQUA LGM glacier database (Ehlers and Gibbard, 2004a)
outputs: Fixed boundary condition datasets loaded into simulation environment

steps.description: Initialize the glacier-climate model described in Allen et al. (2007a) with initial parameter values for melt factors (degree day factors for snow and ice) and hypsometric profile.
steps.searchable_summary: Initialize glacier-climate model with starting parameters.

steps.description: Iteratively adjust degree day factors and hypsometric profile using a defined process (melt factors by 0.1 mm/day/°C and hypsometric profile by 10 m per iteration).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform iterative adjustment of glacier model parameters.
inputs: Current parameter values (degree day factors, hypsometric profile)
Adjustment increments (0.1 mm/day/°C for melt factors, 10 m for profile)

steps.description: Simulate glacier mass balance conditions using the glacier-climate model with the adjusted parameters and the fixed boundary conditions (INQUA LGM glacier database and HadCM3 LGM climate anomalies).
steps.searchable_summary: Run glacier model simulation with current parameters and boundary conditions.
inputs: Fixed boundary conditions (INQUA LGM glacier database, HadCM3 LGM climate anomalies)
outputs: Simulated glacier mass balance results (e.g., surface mass balance)

steps.description: Check if the simulated surface mass balance conditions represent equilibrium (zero mass balance).
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate simulation output against equilibrium criteria.
outputs: Boolean result indicating if equilibrium is reached

steps.description: Repeat steps 3-5 until the equilibrium surface mass balance condition is simulated by HadCM3 LGM climate anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: Loop parameter adjustment and simulation until convergence to equilibrium.
outputs: Final optimized parameter values when equilibrium is reached

steps.description: Record the final optimized melt factors (ranging from 0.1 to 2 mm/day/°C for snow and 0.5 to 3 mm/day/°C for ice) and the required elevation adjustments for the LGM glacier profiles (ranging from 400 m to 900 m).
Estimate the total area of the glacier using the simulated size of the accumulation area and an AAR value of 0.67, which is a commonly used value for present day mountain glaciers.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate total glacier area from simulated accumulation area and AAR.
